weather_line: Northeast winds, duck-scented · compass readings unreliable until further notice

stories.headline: Duck Bandit Code Partially Broken: Two Characters Resolved; They Spell a Direction; Steve Speaks His Hypothesis Aloud at Last
body: At eight forty-seven Sunday morning, Christine set down her tea, looked at the seven-character note for the fourth time in as many days, and announced that the second and fifth characters were the same glyph — and that glyph, under the Weasley Chromatic Cipher (discontinued 1987, archived but not destroyed), resolves to the letter R. The room was quiet for a moment. Then Steve said what he had been thinking since Thursday.

His hypothesis, now spoken aloud for the record, is as follows: the Duck Bandit is not a stranger. The colour sequence — amber, green, red — is a known shorthand among a particular subset of off-road enthusiasts operating in the northeastern highlands. The six-inch interval is a spacing convention. The brass ducks' unnamed direction is not unnamed at all; the household compass simply lacks the notation. 'It's a greeting,' Steve said, 'from someone who knows where we've been.' He did not say how he knew this. He said it the way he says things he has known for a while.

Christine noted that this leaves five characters unresolved and the sender's identity still theoretical. She has begun a second grid. The amber, green, and red ducks are undisturbed on the bonnet, still facing the direction the compass will not name. A fourth duck has not yet appeared. This is being treated as meaningful.

stories.headline: Sunday Morning Declares Itself Unhurried; the Household Receives This News With Varying Degrees of Preparedness
body: The Sunday morning arrived at its customary hour and, finding no agenda waiting for it, proceeded at its own pace. The kitchen table, which has now enjoyed several days of relative order, accepted the morning's presence with the ease of a surface that has remembered what it is for.

Conditions across the household were assessed at nine o'clock as follows: settled, with localised warmth in the lap-adjacent zones, and one ongoing investigation into a coded note that the morning was not going to hurry.

stories.headline: Duty Goblin Attempts Sunday Correspondence; Is Advised That Sunday Is, in Fact, Sunday; Acknowledges This; Sends the Correspondence Anyway
body: For the second weekend running, the duty goblin at Gringotts Branch Seven drafted and dispatched formal correspondence on a day the institution does not officially recognise as a working day. The memo, which concerned a sub-clause adjustment to the Luminous Unattended Items Accord, reached Christine's enchanted in-tray at ten past nine. She read it. She noted the sub-clause was already adjusted — had been, in fact, since Tuesday the fourteenth. She replied in three words. The duty goblin has filed the reply under 'Anticipated.'
The corridor's second brass plaque was observed to be perfectly level this morning. The first plaque offered no comment but was, by all accounts, gratified.

stories.headline: Ministry Confirms Sunday Is Occurring; Notes With Interest That the Week Ahead Has Seven Days; Attaches the Duck Photograph Again, Cropped Differently
body: The Ministry of Magic issued its Sunday bulletin at nine-thirty, confirming that Sunday was proceeding as expected and that the coming week would consist of seven days in the conventional sequence. The bulletin described this information as 'forward-looking.' An attachment was included: the photograph of the red duck, which first appeared in Saturday's bulletin, here cropped to exclude the bonnet but include, in the lower left corner, what may be a footprint. The Ministry did not annotate the footprint. It is not clear they have noticed it.
The Prophet's Creature Intelligence desk has requested the original, uncropped image. The Ministry replied that the original is 'under review.' This is, at minimum, something.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was observed studying the coded note from a distance of approximately four feet for eleven minutes this morning before departing the room at a pace that suggested she had learned what she needed. She has not returned to the note. She has not needed to.

Ellie: Discovered her reflection in the dark screen of the enchanted wireless, greeted it warmly, waited for it to say something, and — when it did not — concluded it was simply having a quiet Sunday. She found this relatable and settled nearby to keep it company.

Jersey: Conducted a thorough morning survey of available laps, selected the warmest with the precision of an instrument calibrated specifically for this purpose, and has not moved. The lap question, for Jersey, is not a dispute. It is a conclusion.
founding_editor_note: That footprint in the Ministry's photograph — I'd check the tread. A man's boot leaves a heel mark. A duck leaves nothing. Whatever that is, it's neither. — D.

in_brief.sport: Sunday lifts reported as 'good' by one party and 'observed approvingly' by another; no further comment was offered, which is the strongest possible endorsement.
in_brief.forecast: Mild and duck-adjacent through the afternoon; the compass will resume normal service once it works out what northeast means from here.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit Stirs Again
storyline_updates.summary: Day 4: Christine resolved two characters of the seven-character note via the Weasley Chromatic Cipher (discontinued 1987) — both the same glyph, resolving to R. Steve spoke his hypothesis aloud: the Bandit is not a stranger, the colour sequence and six-inch interval are known conventions among northeastern highland off-road enthusiasts, and the brass ducks' unnamed direction is a greeting from someone who knows where they've been. Five characters remain unresolved. The Ministry's recropped photograph contains what may be a footprint in the lower left corner — unattributed, unannotated, possibly significant. Lucy studied the note for eleven minutes and departed satisfied. No fourth duck has appeared yet; its absence is being treated as meaningful. Day 5 should resolve the arc: either the remaining five characters decode to a name or a place (or both), the fourth duck arrives and completes the greeting, or the footprint is identified and the Bandit revealed — someone known, fond, and operating from affection rather than mischief. The resolution should be warm and satisfying without being sentimental.
